insight: SuperClaude architecture is sound — STRONG on 4/7 Anthropic internal techniques (DO/DON'T, Verification, Turn Strategy, Skills Budget). Gaps are content-level (numeric criteria, enumeration, gotchas) not structural.

insight: T3 fix: Position 2 (add top-3 critical rules to agent gotchas) is right-sized. Position 3 (hook-based rule reminder) deferred as code change with unclear ROI.

insight: Prohibition + form enumeration > prohibition alone. Anthropic enumerates all evasion forms (prose/summary/structured output fabrication). SuperClaude R15 says cite evidence but does not enumerate HOW agents evade.
author: ajitta
context: CC source: do not fabricate in prose, summary, OR structured output — each form explicitly listed

insight: 7 files committed (4 modified + 3 new docs), 626 insertions, 4 deletions. Tests: 1682/1682 pass (1 pre-existing fail). 2 self-reviews ran (37 + 50 tool calls). 0 regressions.

insight: Self-review catches design-vs-implementation drift: missing R15 examples row and anti_misunderstanding wording inconsistency. When design defines rule text + examples table changes in same section, secondary item is easy to miss.
author: ajitta

insight: Deferred W1 (agent gotchas across 18 agents) and W2 (context:fork on skills) after reviewer challenge: content improvements without measured problems. Start with measurement, not assumption.
author: ajitta
context: W1: no evidence of rule drift in production. W2: current skills dont benefit from fork overhead.

insight: Full brainstorm-to-commit pipeline validated: brainstorm → research → discovery → self-review (corrections) → design → implement → self-review (catch drift) → commit. 2 reviews caught 5 critical + 2 important issues before commit.
author: ajitta
context: ~280K tokens across main + 4 sub-agents. Pipeline prevents factual errors from reaching commit.

insight: W1 Phase 1: 5 agents gotchas added (self-review, system-architect, refactoring-expert, project-manager, root-cause-analyst). Gotchas count 5→10. 720/721 tests pass (1 pre-existing). 7 files changed.
author: ajitta

insight: R06 Scope chosen as secondary gotcha for 3/5 agents (self-review, system-architect, refactoring-expert). Scope creep is the most common cross-cutting agent failure mode, even though R06 was not in the design spec critical rule list (R02, R13, R15, R18).
author: ajitta
context: Design spec template mapped each agent category to one primary rule. R06 added as secondary based on observed agent behavior patterns.

insight: simplicity-guide.md places gotchas before handoff, deviating from agent-authoring.md convention (after handoff, before bounds). backend-architect follows convention correctly. Not corrected to avoid scope creep.
author: ajitta
context: agent-authoring.md says placement: after handoff, before bounds. simplicity-guide has gotchas→handoff→bounds order.

insight: Content-only framework changes (markdown gotchas) have near-zero test risk. All 720 structural tests pass without modification. Plan-to-implementation for content changes takes minutes vs hours for code changes.
author: ajitta
ajitta
insight: W1 Phase 2 selective: 5 agents gotchas added (python-expert, devops-architect, performance-engineer, business-panel-experts, requirements-analyst). Count 10->15. 720/721 tests pass. 7 files committed.

author: ajitta
insight: Phase 2 selective (5/13) over full rollout. Measurement gate impractical (no A/B for compaction drift), token cost trivial (~3 lines/agent), but educators+executors are low-judgment — gotchas have diminishing returns on procedural agents.
context: Original plan said measure Phase 1 first. Pragmatic decision: skip impractical gate, apply to high-value subset only.

author: ajitta
insight: R02 wording must be domain-differentiated even when the rule is identical: "Before implementing" (python) vs "Before configuring" (devops) vs "Before optimizing" (perf). Boilerplate R02 caught by self-review agent in iteration 2.
action: Changed devops-architect R02 from "Before implementing" to "Before configuring...infrastructure isn't already provisioned"

author: ajitta
insight: Self-review loop (--loop iteration 2) caught near-identical R02 wording for python/devops that wouldn't fail any test. Review agents find quality gaps invisible to structural validation.
context: Tests validate structure (tag presence, placement) but not content quality (differentiation, domain specificity).

author: ajitta
insight: R18 Necessity Test applied to requirements-analyst with domain-adapted phrasing: "more complete" replaces "safer/better" as the insufficient justification example. Rule paraphrasing should match agent domain vocabulary.
CLAUDE.md audit: 3 stale claims fixed (MCP 9->8, tests 1787->1807, install chain +2 files). 12 orphan root files deleted (4,209 lines). Score 87->94.
author: ajitta

insight: Glob tool returns different file counts than ls | wc -l on Windows Git Bash — backslash path separators cause undercounting with pipe chains. Glob is the reliable source.
author: ajitta

insight: CLAUDE.md staleness accumulates silently after cleanup commits. MorphLLM removal (e18212f) and test additions were committed but doc counts never updated — no CI or hook enforces CLAUDE.md currency.
author: ajitta

insight: Current content inventory: 33 commands, 23 agents, 5 skills, 7 modes, 8 MCP docs, 4 core configs. Test collection: 1,807. Version: 4.4.1+ajitta.

ajitta
insight: Authoring rule compliance fix: 57 files, +303/-71 lines. Tests 1266->1267 (frontend-architect fixed). Gotchas 100% coverage across agents/commands/skills
context: Full audit and fix of all 4 authoring rule sets (agent, command, skill, mode)
action: Script-based bulk edit + manual fixes for structural violations

author: ajitta
insight: Sub-agents with bypassPermissions mode still denied Edit tool on Windows — permissions enforced by host, not agent mode. Python script workaround required
context: 4 sub-agent attempts (auto + bypassPermissions) all failed to Edit files. Main conversation Edit works fine.

author: ajitta
insight: maxTurns removed from all 22 agents (R18: system not broken without it, values were untested estimates, CC has built-in limits)
context: Originally added to all 23 agents, then removed after R18 necessity test. Only effort field retained.
action: sed removal of maxTurns line from all agent frontmatter

author: ajitta
insight: 3 Opus agents (backend-architect, devops-architect, refactoring-expert) effort raised medium->high — model routing rationale (judgment tasks) must match effort level
context: Self-review flagged mismatch: Opus used for judgment but effort capped at medium. User chose option 1 (raise to high).

author: ajitta
insight: Effort/model alignment rule: Opus agents should have effort >= high. Sonnet agents should have effort <= medium. Mismatch signals configuration error
context: Derived from reviewing all 23 agents. Opus is chosen for judgment tasks that need deep reasoning — constraining effort contradicts the routing decision.

author: ajitta
insight: Leaked CC source TypeScript type shows 1-5|EffortValue union for effort field, but runtime YAML parser only accepts strings (low/medium/high/max). Type definition != runtime behavior
context: User asked to verify claim from leaked source. Web research + project history (cc-source-alignment-discovery spec SUPERSEDED note) confirm strings only at runtime.

author: ajitta
insight: Python batch script outperforms sub-agent delegation for bulk mechanical edits (52 files in seconds vs 4 failed sub-agent attempts over ~15 min)
context: fix_authoring.py handled all agent+command edits reliably. Sub-agents failed due to Edit permission issues on Windows.

author: chosh1179
insight: PostToolUse test dispatch extended by path convention (agents/commands/skills/modes .md → structure tests) over extension heuristic. Existing hook skipped .md entirely; path-based routing reuses the same hook without a second dispatcher.

author: chosh1179
insight: superclaude audit --verbose silently suppressed drift per-file details. Verbose flag wired for cross_refs/usage sections only, not drift — flag existence != verbose output coverage.

author: chosh1179
insight: Hook script sync requires both make deploy (CLI via editable) AND superclaude install --force (hook scripts). Editable install does not cover files copied to ~/.claude/superclaude/scripts/. Silent divergence source — script edits look deployed but arent active.

author: chosh1179
insight: Skipped 2b (test-driven YAML refactor loop) per [R18]. 1339/1339 structure tests already passing — no refactor backlog to iterate against. Insights report described a future workflow, not a current gap.

author: ajitta
insight: Full XML conversion of .claude/rules/*-authoring.md rejected; YAML sidecar extraction chosen.
context: 3 enum duplications + 1 red-color drift bug justify R18; 820+ lines of pedagogical prose fail necessity test.

author: ajitta
insight: Anthropic "No XML tags anywhere" ban is frontmatter-scoped only (name+description), not body-level.
context: PDF checklist wording is ambiguous tutorial guidance, not API rule. src/superclaude/** XML bodies are not violations.

author: ajitta
insight: VALID_COLORS permitted red but no agent used it and role-group table had no red entry.
context: Latent drift caught during duplication audit, not by any test. Removing red and loading colors from schemas.yaml fixed both the drift and the SSOT.

author: ajitta
insight: Sub-agent overconfident claim (Anthropic explicitly forbids) forced to NUANCED after verification challenge.
context: Always cross-verify strong external-claim findings via SendMessage + direct WebFetch before acting on sub-agent research.

author: ajitta
insight: uv run pytest fails with Failed to canonicalize script path on this Windows setup; python -m pytest works identically.
context: Use direct python when uv run misbehaves on Windows. Root cause not investigated — workaround sufficient for now.

author: ajitta
insight: --loop terminated at iteration 2. Generalized drift guard deferred (R18 YAGNI).
context: self-review flagged it as low-priority optional; current 4 guard tests cover main drift vectors with no failure evidence.

author: ajitta
insight: Rule citation precision [R06] scope-defense vs [R18] necessity-test — initial blind R18 tagging on 5 gotchas reverted to R06 in commit 10 after self-review 2nd pass.
context: content-not-service/cli-simplicity/no-frontend/rich-only/false-positive are scope-defensive (SC is not a web service/UI) not necessity-gating. R18 is for unsolicited code proposals.

author: ajitta
insight: Simulation-based audit via 5 parallel sub-agents (22 agents × 5 scenarios) reveals cross-agent inconsistencies (trigger collisions, mindset-gotcha conflicts) invisible to per-agent review.
context: 110 scenarios produced P0-P3 finding tree. Per-agent review had previously reached 100% gotcha coverage without catching cross-cutting issues.

author: ajitta
insight: install-tree-boundary near-violation — P3 Threshold Standardization originally proposed schemas.yaml extraction; user corrected mid-recommendation. .claude/rules/** is NOT shipped to ~/.claude/.
context: Runtime agents read src/superclaude/** only. Dev-time files (schemas.yaml, .claude/rules/authoring.md) are test-time enforcement only. Design pivoted to RULES.md canonical block + schemas validation test.

author: ajitta
insight: R06/R18 Uniform Citation original proposal rescoped mid-execution — strict uniformity (23/23) replaced with natural gotcha→rule mapping (20/23: R02/R06/R15/R18). 3 agents (git-workflow/insight-analyst/socratic-mentor) skipped because their gotchas dont map to framework rules.
context: Right-sized over uniform: forced tags create noise. Rescoping acknowledged in commit message, reviewer validated.

author: ajitta
insight: self-review agent is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob/Agent) — cannot run Bash tests. R15 verification must be performed by invoker; sub-agents can only do static analysis.
context: Reviewer explicitly stated Verification not possible: no Bash tool available in this review session. User ran pytest fresh and cited 831/831. Pattern: delegation ≠ verification.

author: ajitta
insight: User one-line correction (rules/schemas.yaml 이용 불가) pivoted entire P3 design. Accuracy-based user feedback is the highest-leverage loop input.
context: Initial recommendation violated install-tree-boundary gotcha (which is in .claude/rules/gotchas/authoring.md). Without user catch, P3 proposal would have produced runtime-inaccessible configuration.

author: ajitta
insight: Effort policy pivot: from model-tier alignment (Apr 12) to omit-by-default inheriting parent session. 12 agents pruned, 11 kept where domain forces deviation.
context: Parent /effort xhigh now propagates to all non-explicit agents instead of being capped by rigid medium/high frontmatter.

author: ajitta
insight: Explicit effort keep-criteria: low=mechanical (git-workflow/repo-index), high=domain-forced deep reasoning (architects/security/root-cause), max=multi-perspective synthesis. Everything else inherits.
context: medium is the default and is almost always redundant as explicit value. Codified in .claude/rules/agent-authoring.md table.

author: ajitta
insight: xhigh not adopted despite CC 2.1.111+ and Opus 4.7 support — no concrete usage evidence that existing high agents underperform.
context: Consistent with P1 A3 decision (max->xhigh downgrade also deferred without usage feedback). Add only with evidence.

author: ajitta
insight: Prior Opus>=high alignment rule (2026-04-12T02:30:04+09:00) superseded — Opus agents like refactoring-expert now omit effort entirely and rely on parent session /effort.
context: The Apr 12 rule assumed rigid per-agent effort; the new model treats parent session as SSOT for depth preference.

author: ajitta
insight: User restraint principle for frontmatter: lower if safe, remove if doubtful, add only when clearly needed. Generalizable to other optional fields.
context: User phrasing: effort를 명확하게 하양해도 성능 저하 없으면 적용, 의심스러우면 빼자, 명백히 xhigh 이상 써야 하면 넣자.

author: ajitta
insight: <mcp servers="..."> XML tag had 0 runtime effect and 0 actual usage across 23 agents — pure dead pattern.
context: agent-authoring.md template had it; audit.py parsed it; test_cross_references.py validated it. All operating on empty set. Removed from all 3 layers.

author: ajitta
insight: Rejected forcing generic <focus>/<actions> template onto 4 domain-specialized agents (deep-researcher/socratic-mentor/simplicity-guide/project-manager).
context: Domain-specific tags like <strategies>, <domains>, <hop_patterns> carry higher information density than generic template slots. Uniformity < information density when semantics differ.

author: ajitta
insight: Template-layer dead pattern removal requires 3-way cleanup: template guide + audit script + test validator.
context: Removing <mcp> from agent-authoring.md alone would leave audit.py parsing + 128 skip-tests as silent dead code. Complete removal demands all three layers coherent.

author: ajitta
insight: test_cross_references.py runtime 1.55s to 0.15s after removing 128 dead-skip MCP tests (10x speedup).
context: 128 tests always skipped (no <mcp> usage) were still instantiated by pytest fixtures. Mass-skip count is a signal of potentially removable code paths.

author: ajitta
insight: User "실측해보자" preference — treat documented claims as unverified until empirically tested.
context: Documentation said <mcp> is "Documentation-only". User demanded empirical verification, which immediately surfaced that the tag has zero usage and zero runtime effect. Proactive measurement beats post-hoc trust.

author: ajitta
insight: 18-trial A/B (3 prompts x 3 reps x 2 conditions): <component> wrapper only detectable effect is literal name=... quoting in self-ID. Bounds-awareness + task behavior identical across conditions (token use, tool calls, pattern depth within noise).

author: ajitta
insight: CC agents registered at session start only — new files in ~/.claude/agents/ not available via Task subagent_type until restart. Blocks same-session A/B iteration on agent files.

author: ajitta
insight: Inline-body proxy: when agent hot-reload blocked, embed full agent definition in general-purpose subagent prompt to isolate content-format variables. Imperfect (diverges from CC system-prompt injection path) but surfaces format-level effects.

author: ajitta
insight: <component> wrapper policy split by content type: modes/mcp KEEP (sole identity source — no frontmatter), agents/skills/commands REMOVE candidate (frontmatter name + filename already provide identity). ~150-line reduction possible across ~60 files + 3 test classes.

author: ajitta
insight: Verification-replay surfaces pre-existing gaps: re-running Part A V1 at Tier 2 caught NEVER use Write tool at insight.md:107 (commit b5c974f3, 2026-04-11) missed by Tier 1 walk.
context: Lint checklists earn most of their value on the second run, not the first. Replay catches drift AND missed cases from the initial authoring pass.

author: ajitta
insight: T2-b implemented as 1-line doc note in <sub_agent_decision>, not threshold rewrite. R18 passes for doc note (D6 user-facing impact documented) but fails for threshold changes (needs eval data).
context: Smallest reversible step when evidence is partial. Doc note surfaces the under-spawn caveat without committing to numeric threshold changes that require behavioral measurement.

author: ajitta
insight: Blanket grep lints yield false positives on gotcha language — V1 NEVER pattern catches both aggressive enforcement prompting and legitimate tool-safety gotchas with same syntactic signature.
context: Future V1 refinement options: (a) exclude NEVER use <Tool> tool patterns inside <gotchas> blocks, (b) reword the gotchas for syntactic cleanliness, or (c) accept as documented exception.

author: ajitta
insight: Two-layer verification distinction from 시물레이션/측정 directive: (a) content change landed — grep/pytest, (b) underlying behavioral claim true — model eval. Different tools, different costs, different confidence.
context: Tier 2 executed layer (a) only; layer (b) deferred to Part B. Extends 2026-04-17T02:45:04 실측해보자 principle by naming the layer boundary explicitly.

author: ajitta
insight: T2-a chose Option 3 (guidance in agent-authoring.md) over Option 2 (add effort: xhigh to 5 coding agents). Preserves inherit-by-default policy from commit 8edd05d.
context: Direct echo of 2026-04-17T01:45:04 user restraint principle (lower if safe, remove if doubtful, add only when clearly needed) — now applied to xhigh tier, not just medium. Consistent with Apr 17 xhigh-not-adopted decision.

author: ajitta
insight: Opus 4.7 aesthetic default is domain-conditional — editorial briefs reproduce cream/serif/terracotta (exactly #F4EFE6 + Canela + #8B2E2E); technical briefs (fintech/dashboard/landing) produce dark + Inter instead. T1-a <aesthetics> guard already correctly scopes by domain.
context: 3 technical briefs all produced #0B1220 or #0F1419 dark navy + Inter. 1 editorial brief (literary magazine) produced exact literal match to doc claim. No refinement to frontend-architect.md needed.

author: ajitta
insight: Identical buggy Python input, 2 sub-agents: 'only important' → 10 findings vs 'report every' → 38 findings (3.8× delta). Severity mix shifts 2C/3H/3M/2L → 5C/7H/11M/9L/6nits. Empirically validates <finding_policy>.
context: T1-b block in quality-engineer/self-review/security-engineer agents is load-bearing — literal Opus 4.7 filtering on 'be concise' style prompts strips ~75% of legitimate findings.

author: ajitta
insight: Behavioral verification via parallel general-purpose sub-agents with output-only prompts resolved B5 ambiguity in 3 iterations: iter1 contradicted doc, iter2 ruled out prompt noise with 2 more technical briefs, iter3 found reproduction in editorial brief. Convergence signal: new brief yields no new signal.
context: Extends 2026-04-17T03:15:07 inline-body proxy pattern with explicit loop-convergence criterion. Paired prompts must return artifact only (no commentary) for clean A/B scoring.

author: ajitta
insight: Framework audit artifacts belong in docs/reports/, not .claude/rules/checklists/. .claude/rules/ reserved for authoring guides (test-enforced) + gotchas (CC-loaded). Retroactively applied via git mv + 13-ref update in discovery spec + tier-1/2 plans.
context: Commit d11dc10. Motivated by RULES.md <doc_output_convention> which lists docs/reports/ for living audit docs. .claude/rules/checklists/ had no documented purpose and no test/CC hook.

author: ajitta
insight: Tier 1 blindly followed discovery spec §7 file-location directive (.claude/rules/checklists/), violating <doc_output_convention> in RULES.md. User catch: '왜 너는 rules/checklists 에 파일을 쓰는 거지?' surfaced the drift. Rule: Tier N implementations must sanity-check spec's paths against framework conventions, not follow blindly.
context: {trigger: spec §7 prescribed path, misread: followed without convention check, actual_intent: living audit doc → docs/reports/ per <doc_output_convention>, violated_rule: R14 (Correction Capture), prevention: before creating a file at a spec-prescribed path, run ls + grep on <doc_output_convention> to confirm the directory's documented role}

author: ajitta
insight: Part B behavioral tests executed: B3/B5/B6/B7 direct + B4/B2 proxy = 6/8. B1 (effort strictness), B8 (thinking-token scaling) require cross-session / telemetry access, not sub-agent runnable. B4 subagent under-trigger observable only by main-model. Must externalize to eval harness.
context: Closes 2026-04-18T19:45:03 two-layer verification loop — layer (b) behavioral tests now 75% complete. Remaining 25% (B1/B8) flagged as external-harness follow-ups in docs/reports/OPUS_4_7_ALIGNMENT.md Part B summary.

author: ajitta
insight: Any non-command *.md placed under src/superclaude/commands/ is auto-discovered by CC as namespaced slash commands (e.g. /sc:templates:*), polluting the /sc: namespace.
context: Initial Gap 4 impl put docs-scaffold templates at commands/templates/docs-scaffold/. After superclaude install, CC listed sc:templates:docs-scaffold:PRD etc. as invocable slash commands. Fix: new install component at sibling src/superclaude/templates/ to superclaude/templates/. Not caught by 1,767-test green run — only surfaced by manual install + skill-list inspection.

author: ajitta
insight: Hook runtime state files need explicit uninstaller cleanup — they are not install-time artifacts, so uninstall_all misses them by default.
context: loop_guard_state.json was left in .claude/ after uninstall --scope project. uninstall_all only walks install-time files (hooks/hooks.json etc.). Added runtime-state removal block alongside hooks.json removal in install_inventory.py.

author: ajitta
insight: Gap 3 (headless phase executor) parked with design spec instead of implementing. Applied R18 Necessity Test: no concrete context-rot failure case today, so MVS is premature abstraction.
context: Video showed claude -p subprocess pattern; initial analysis flagged large design surface. Delivered docs/specs/headless-phase-executor-design-ajitta-2026-04-21.md with 6 decisions requiring user sign-off (entry point, phase format, subprocess model, state, git discipline, failure modes) rather than plunging into implementation.

author: ajitta
insight: /sc:implement --loop iter-2 caught install-path pollution during manual deploy verification, NOT during 1,767-test green run. Tests verified layout; only CC skill-menu inspection revealed slash-command discovery behavior.
context: Test suite asserted templates land at commands/sc/templates/docs-scaffold/ — which was technically correct but semantically wrong. Generalization: for any change touching install paths or CC-discovered namespaces (commands/, agents/, skills/), manual post-install smoke is non-negotiable even with full test pass.

author: ajitta
insight: For headless-executor MVS, chose CCs context: fork pattern (existing SC infra) over claude -p subprocess — reuse-before-add-dependency principle.
context: claude -p matches the video exactly but adds: CC binary on PATH, credential handoff, Windows shell quoting, cross-process debugging. context: fork already used by SC skills, same-process, structured IO. Subprocess mode deferred as --headless=subprocess upgrade path if demand appears.

author: ajitta
insight: when_to_use field deprecated upstream — fold triggers into description (canonical pattern from Anthropic skill-reviewer:57). Reverses v1 rename-hyphen-to-underscore approach.
context: v1 spec proposed renaming when-to-use -> when_to_use. Self-review found Anthropic skill-reviewer agent labels when_to_use deprecated; canonical skill-development uses neither form (triggers folded into description with third-person voice).

author: ajitta
insight: Bare common abbreviations in skill descriptions trigger auto-invoke false positives. "VS" in verbalized-sampling matched "X vs Y" comparisons. Trigger tokens must be specific — flag-prefixed (--vs) safe; bare 2-letter acronyms risky.
context: Canary re-test confirmed: "should I use Postgres vs DynamoDB" triggered VS skill before fix. After tightening (removed bare "VS", added explicit "X vs Y is NOT a trigger"), no false positive while "give me multiple perspectives" still triggers correctly.

author: ajitta
insight: Hook regex substring matching wrongly blocks safer flag alternatives. 'git push -f' substring-matches 'git push -force-with-lease' (the SAFER option). Use negative char classes like '-f([^a-zA-Z]|$)' to fake word-boundaries.
context: Found in 3 places: ship/SKILL.md hook, finishing-a-development-branch/SKILL.md hook, hooks/hooks.json Bash safety. Same bug pattern: --force substring inside --force-with-lease, -f substring inside -force-with-lease, git branch -D substring inside hypothetical -DEBUG. Fix preserves block intent while explicitly allowing --force-with-lease.

author: ajitta
insight: 'claude -p "<prompt>" --output-format json' enables programmatic skill auto-invoke canary probes. Each call spawns a fresh session — covers reproducibility intrinsically. JSON output exposes skill activation in 'result' field.
context: Used for PR1 D3 acceptance gate when interactive fresh-session testing was not available from automation context. Ran 11 scenarios (positive triggers, paraphrase, negative controls, disable-model-invocation gating, explicit invocation, reproducibility) in parallel via Bash. All minimum merge gates green. Note: slash commands like /simplicity-coach get mangled by Git Bash quoting.

author: ajitta
insight: Negative-instruction phrasing inside skill descriptions narrows triggers without removing keywords. "Do NOT trigger for X (X is NOT a trigger)" is respected by Claude. Useful for pruning false-positive overlap while keeping legitimate matches.
context: Verified empirically: verbalized-sampling description ending with "comparisons phrased as X vs Y (the bare word vs alone is a comparison preposition, NOT a trigger)" stopped false-positive on Postgres-vs-DynamoDB query but preserved true-positive on "multiple perspectives". Same pattern works in B3 ("what is 2+2?" not triggered despite VS keywords).

author: ajitta
insight: CC parser uses literal mixed-case key matching for skill frontmatter: kebab-case (disable-model-invocation, allowed-tools, argument-hint, user-invocable), snake_case (only when_to_use, deprecated), single-word (name, description, model, effort, paths). Reflexive kebab-casing breaks parsing — exact key required.
context: Leaked CC source (leehanchung.github.io) reads frontmatter.when_to_use via JS dot-notation while frontmatter["allowed-tools"] uses bracket-notation. No consistent style. Project bias toward kebab-case caused the silent when-to-use bug. Documented in skill-authoring.md "Naming taxonomy" subsection.

author: ajitta
insight: 'make deploy' only installs the CLI tool (uv tool install --editable). Skills require separate 'superclaude install --force' to propagate to ~/.claude/skills/. Two-step deploy path.
context: Bit the PR1 canary probe at first run — verified ~/.claude/skills/confidence-check/SKILL.md still had old when-to-use content even after make deploy. Required explicit superclaude install --force to propagate src/ edits to user scope. CLAUDE.md says make deploy reflects src/ changes immediately, which is true for the CLI but NOT for installed skills/agents/commands.

author: ajitta
insight: Enforce self-review as hard-block gate after /sc:brainstorm — soft recommendation insufficient. Single source session: self-review caught 3 critical reversals on user-approved spec (when_to_use deprecated, install-path '3 targets' wrong, D2 sunk-cost). Pattern reproduced in this session's plan review (4 more critical findings).
context: User-approved decisions still ship with errors when delegated to model judgment. Soft 'consider running /sc:review' is ignored under time pressure or auto-mode.
action: Coded hard-block in src/superclaude/commands/brainstorm.md flow 5b + gotcha + bounds + handoff (review listed before plan). C1 phase commit c438fa3.

author: ajitta
insight: CC project memory dir path encoding has off-by-one trap. C:\\Users\\... → C--Users-... (drive colon + backslash collapse together to --). Naive replace order ':' → '--' then '\\' → '-' produces C---Users-... (extra dash). Correct encoding: normalize \\ → /, then ':/' → '--', then remaining '/' → '-'.
context: Hit during Phase 6 (memory_staleness hook) TDD red phase — encoding test failed with C---Users assertion mismatch.
action: Fixed in src/superclaude/scripts/memory_staleness.py encode_project_path(); 9/9 unit tests now green.

author: ajitta
insight: TDD exemption for markdown-only phases must be explicitly invoked in plan header. CLAUDE.md 'Markdown-only changes carry no test risk' exemption exists, but if plan does not state which phases skip failing-test-first, self-review flags it as TDD violation. Add 'TDD applicability: Phases X,Y,Z markdown-only skip per CLAUDE.md' line.
context: Self-review audit (round 2) found N1 — 'No phase is genuinely TDD' — even though Markdown-only edits are exempt. Plan never invoked the exemption, so audit could not distinguish 'skipped intentionally' from 'forgot TDD'.
action: Added explicit TDD applicability line to plan header (docs/plans/retrospective-followups-ajitta-2026-04-25.md).

author: ajitta
insight: Per-skill canary manifest (canary.yaml next to SKILL.md) chosen over centralized in-tree fixture file. Locality benefit: trigger definitions move with skill body on rename/relocation. Iterator fixture globs src/superclaude/skills/**/canary.yaml. Cost: per-skill ceremony for new manifest creation. Net: locality > one-file convenience.
context: Q3 of brainstorm spec — options [a] single fixture-in-tree, [b] per-skill manifest, [c] both. User picked [b]. Subsequent decision: framework fixture deferred per R18 (Necessity Test) — no system breakage without it.
action: Implemented in tests/integration/test_skill_canary.py + 2 reference manifests (confidence-check, verbalized-sampling). A1 phase commit 1959698.

author: ajitta
insight: Memory verified: field shipped as minimal stub (per-entry SessionStart scan); broader systemic curation skill (quarterly re-verification of all time-sensitive memory) deferred to separate brainstorm. Scope discipline preserves A2's detection value without absorbing systemic-design overhead.
context: Source §4.3 retrospective proposed 'quarterly memory-curation skill' as fix; spec narrowed to per-entry verified: field + SessionStart hook (~80 LOC, pure stdlib). Avoids designing the curation skill as part of this bundle.
action: Implemented A2 as src/superclaude/scripts/memory_staleness.py + hooks.json registration + verified: convention in hooks/README.md. Phase 6 commit 01a520b.

author: ajitta
insight: /sc:plan defaults to Phase N framing; PR N is opt-in via --pr-bundle flag. Reality: most multi-step plans execute as N phase commits on 1 branch (single review cycle), not as N separate PRs. Default naming should match reality. Source §5.1: prior 4-PR plan was actually 4 phase commits on 1 branch.
context: Q5 of brainstorm spec resolved to [b] phase-default + opt-in PR. Plan plan.md updated: syntax adds [--pr-bundle], flow step 3 uses Phase, examples + gotchas explain when to opt-in.
action: Updated src/superclaude/commands/plan.md (Phase 8 commit b8dc312).

author: ajitta
insight: Makefile fix for uv canonicalize bug (B1: uv run pytest → uv run python -m pytest) does not auto-propagate to PostToolUse test_runner_hook.py. The hook bypasses Makefile and shells out to 'uv run pytest' directly, reproducing the Windows canonicalize-script-path false-positive on every Edit/Write to .md files. Both invocation sites must be fixed independently.
context: Phase 1 fixed Makefile but hook noise persisted through entire 8-phase implementation. Surfaced as repeating 'Tests FAILED ... canonicalize script path' system-reminders during Edit operations.
action: Fixed src/superclaude/scripts/test_runner_hook.py (2 sites: AUTHORING_TEST_MAP detection + detect_test_command fallback) → 'uv run python -m pytest'. Bonus commit f412615.
